steps.title: Isolate the radical
steps.content:
Start by subtracting $28$ from both sides so the square root term is alone. This keeps the equation balanced while preparing to undo the square root.
$$\sqrt{w}+28=34$$
$$\sqrt{w}=6$$

steps.title: Square both sides
steps.content: Square both sides to solve for $w$. $$(\sqrt{w})^2=6^2$$ $$w=36$$ Substituting $36$ back into the original equation gives $6+28=34$, so it works. The answer is D.
